---
title: "Benchmarking_Replicates"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(SpatialExperiment)
library(spatstat.geom)
library(spatstat.random)
library(spatstat.explore)
library(spicyR)
library(TreeBH)
library(DART)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(furrr)
source("R/globals.R")
source("R/simulation_helpers.R")
source("R/benchmarking_helpers.R")
source("R/pfilter.R")
```

# Benchmarking Single Sensitivity

```{r}
# =============================================================
# nk_t_by_survival.R
#
# Question: Across replications, what fraction of the time does
# NK__T survive BY correction at the major level?
#
# This script assumes you have already run spicyR at the major
# level for each replication and saved the results as a list of
# spicy objects, e.g. spicyMajorList[[rep]].
#
# Adjust the loading block below to match your actual file paths.
# =============================================================

library(tidyverse)

source("R/globals.R")
source("R/benchmarking_helpers.R")

TARGET_PAIR = "NK__T"   # canonical form (alphabetical)
Q_LEVEL     = 0.05
COND_B      = "signal"

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load your replicated spicy-major results
#    Replace this block with however you saved your outputs.
#    Two common patterns are shown; uncomment the one you use.
# -------------------------------------------------------------

# Pattern A: a single .rds file containing a named list
#   spicyMajorList = readRDS("results/spicy_major_list.rds")

# Pattern B: one .rds file per replication stored in a folder
#   rdsFiles       = list.files("results/major/", pattern = "\\.rds$",
#                                full.names = TRUE)
#   spicyMajorList = lapply(rdsFiles, readRDS)

# For illustration, if you have a single combined results tibble
# produced by run_all_methods(), you can instead extract the
# ClusterTrim / Ours results directly — see the note at the end.

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Core helper: does NK__T survive BY at the major level in
#    one spicy-major result?
# -------------------------------------------------------------

nkT_survives_by = function(spicyMajor, conditionB = COND_B,
                            q = Q_LEVEL, target = TARGET_PAIR) {
  majorDf = extract_major_pvals(spicyMajor, conditionB)

  # BY-adjusted p-values over all major-level pairs
  majorDf = majorDf %>%
    mutate(p_adj_by = p.adjust(p_major, method = "BY"))

  # Does the target pair appear, and is it significant?
  row = majorDf %>% filter(fam == target)

  if (nrow(row) == 0) {
    warning(sprintf("'%s' not found in major-level results.", target))
    return(NA)
  }

  row$p_adj_by[1] <= q
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Apply across all replications
# -------------------------------------------------------------
rdsFiles = list.files(
  "results/simulation/cache/single/spicy/",
  pattern = "^w0\\.90_rep\\d+\\.rds$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

spicyMajorList = lapply(rdsFiles, function(f) readRDS(f)$spicyMajor)
survivalResults = tibble(
  rep      = seq_along(spicyMajorList),
  survives = vapply(spicyMajorList, nkT_survives_by, logical(1))
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Summary
# -------------------------------------------------------------

survivalRate = mean(survivalResults$survives, na.rm = TRUE)
nReps        = sum(!is.na(survivalResults$survives))

cat(sprintf(
  "\n'%s' survives BY correction at the major level in %d / %d replications (%.1f%%)\n\n",
  TARGET_PAIR, sum(survivalResults$survives, na.rm = TRUE),
  nReps, survivalRate * 100
))

print(survivalResults)

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: If you have a combined allResults tibble from
# run_all_methods(), the ClusterTrim and Ours methods already
# gate through BY at the major level. You can audit their
# gate-passing rate directly like so:
#
#   gateRate = allResults %>%
#     filter(method %in% c("ClusterTrim_BY", "Ours")) %>%
#     group_by(rep, method) %>%
#     summarise(passed = any(reject), .groups = "drop") %>%
#     group_by(method) %>%
#     summarise(survivalRate = mean(passed))
#
# This gives the fraction of replications where *any* subtype
# pair was detected — not directly the major-gate survival, but
# it's a useful cross-check.
# -------------------------------------------------------------
```
